nodes.name: 佩里哈山脈
nodes.summary: 佩里哈山脈是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联48种鸟类，包括Band-tailed guan、紫冠雉、Black-fronted wood quail等48种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 孔多爾山脈
nodes.summary: 孔多爾山脈是南美洲的山脈，位於厄瓜多爾和秘魯，屬於安第斯山脈的一部分，全長150公里，面積25,857平方公里，最高點海拔高度2,950米。

nodes.name: 科克萊省
nodes.summary: 科克萊省是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联4种鸟类，包括Rufous-browed tyrannulet、Yellow-throated chlorospingus、Sooty-faced finch等4种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: LC
nodes.summary: LC表示 IUCN 无危等级，当前关联8208种鸟类，如鸵鸟、鸸鹋、南方鶴鴕等8208种鸟类。

nodes.name: 栖息地破坏
nodes.summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。

nodes.name: 瓜納卡斯特山脈
nodes.summary: 瓜納卡斯特山脈是哥斯達黎加的山脈，位於該國北部，毗鄰與尼加拉瓜接壤的邊境，全長110公里，最高點海拔高度2,028米，該山體在第四紀形成。

nodes.name: 常綠森林
nodes.summary: 常綠森林是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联442种鸟类，包括紫冠雉、角冠雉、Razor-billed curassow等442种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: American Ornithological Society
nodes.summary: American Ornithological Society是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联80种鸟类，包括黑頭鴨、绿翅雁、Chaco chachalaca等80种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 達連省
nodes.summary: 達連省是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联18种鸟类，包括乔科穴䳍、Dusky-backed jacamar、Laughing falcon等18种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 博卡斯德爾托羅省
nodes.summary: 博卡斯德爾托羅省是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联5种鸟类，包括Rufous-browed tyrannulet、Mountain thrush、Yellow-throated chlorospingus等5种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 哥斯達黎加中部山脈
nodes.summary: 哥斯達黎加中部山脈是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联14种鸟类，包括靛喉寶石蜂鳥、Costa Rican pygmy owl、Rufous-browed tyrannulet等14种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: Rufous-browed tyrannulet
nodes.summary: Rufous-browed tyrannulet（Rufous-browed tyrannulet，学名 Phylloscartes superciliaris），在图谱中标记为无危（LC）物种，已关联9个分布地点节点，主要威胁包括栖息地破坏。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: It is found in Costa Rica on the Caribbean slope from the Cordillera de Guanacaste to the Cordillera Central and separately in western Panama from Bocas del Toro Province to Coclé Province, also on the Caribbean slope

links.evidence: It is found in Costa Rica on the Caribbean slope from the Cordillera de Guanacaste to the Cordillera Central and separately in western Panama from Bocas del Toro Province to Coclé Province, also on the Caribbean slope

links.evidence: It is found in Costa Rica on the Caribbean slope from the Cordillera de Guanacaste to the Cordillera Central and separately in western Panama from Bocas del Toro Province to Coclé Province, also on the Caribbean slope

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: It is found in Costa Rica on the Caribbean slope from the Cordillera de Guanacaste to the Cordillera Central and separately in western Panama from Bocas del Toro Province to Coclé Province, also on the Caribbean slope

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: palloris is found in eastern Panama's Darién Province and adjacent northwestern Colombia

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: griseocapillus is found in the Serranía del Perijá that straddles the Colombia-Venezuela border, patchily in the northern Colombian Andes, and on the eastern Andean slope in southern Ecuador's Cordillera de Cutucú and Cordillera del Cóndor.{{cite book | last = | first = | title =Check-list of North American Birds | publisher =American Ornithologists' Union |edition =7th | date =1998 | location =Washington, D.C

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: griseocapillus is found in the Serranía del Perijá that straddles the Colombia-Venezuela border, patchily in the northern Colombian Andes, and on the eastern Andean slope in southern Ecuador's Cordillera de Cutucú and Cordillera del Cóndor.{{cite book | last = | first = | title =Check-list of North American Birds | publisher =American Ornithologists' Union |edition =7th | date =1998 | location =Washington, D.C

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: | page =379 }} Some authors extend the Cordillera del Cóndor range of griseocapillus into extreme northern Peru. However, the South American Classification Committee of the American Ornithological Society has only sight records from Peru and therefore treats the rufous-browed tyrannulet as hypothetical in that country. The rufous-browed tyrannulet inhabits the canopy and edges of humid and wet montane evergreen forest in the subtropical zone

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: | page =379 }} Some authors extend the Cordillera del Cóndor range of griseocapillus into extreme northern Peru. However, the South American Classification Committee of the American Ornithological Society has only sight records from Peru and therefore treats the rufous-browed tyrannulet as hypothetical in that country. The rufous-browed tyrannulet inhabits the canopy and edges of humid and wet montane evergreen forest in the subtropical zone

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: No immediate threats have been identified. It is considered "quite uncommon" in Costa Rica, "local and enigmatic" in Colombia, "apparently relatively common" in Venezuela, "local" in Ecuador, and "locally fairly common" in Peru. "Human activity has little short-term direct effect on Rufous-browed Tyrannulet, other than the local effects of habitat destruction."
